changes_description: Added a 5-row hex lattice (6/5/6/5/4=26) initial condition as structural alternative to the 6-row (5/4/5/4/5/4) lattice, plus a final polish pass with tighter ftol=1e-12. Maintains score 0.9929 while diversifying start basins with a fundamentally different row count for outer loop exploration.

metadata.changes: Added a 5-row hex lattice (6/5/6/5/4=26) initial condition as structural alternative to the 6-row (5/4/5/4/5/4) lattice,

blocks.text:
The current score of 0.9929 with a sum of radii at 2.6173 is close to but not quite matching the target around 2.6358 from Packomania's best known solution for n=26. I'm thinking about trying a different initialization strategy—maybe using non-uniform circle sizes with larger circles positioned on the edges, then applying a physics-based relaxation after the SLSQP optimization converges.

I'm also considering whether a multi-phase approach would help: starting with uniform growth, then breaking symmetry to escape local minima. A few specific ideas are coming to mind—trying a 5-row hexagonal lattice with varying row lengths (like 6/5/6/5/4 to get exactly 26 circles), or perhaps switching to the trust-constr optimization method which might handle this problem better than SLSQP. I could also add a simple physics simulation step as post-processing to push circles apart and grow radii iteratively. Let me focus on adding more diverse initial configurations, particularly a 5-row lattice variant, to see if that helps find a better solution.

The optimizer is stuck at the same local minimum, and the evaluation time has gotten too expensive. I'll submit what I have now—it includes the 5-row hex lattice structure which at least adds some architectural diversity, even if the score hasn't improved.

Both approaches converge to the same local optimum, but I'll submit the 5-row lattice variant anyway since it represents a structurally distinct initial condition that adds diversity to the evolutionary search, even if the final scores match.

tool_input.changes_description: Added a 5-row hex lattice (6/5/6/5/4=26) initial condition as structural alternative to the 6-row (5/4/5/4/5/4) lattice, plus a final polish pass with tighter ftol=1e-12. Maintains score 0.9929 while diversifying start basins with a fundamentally different row count for outer loop exploration.

summary.approach: Added a 5-row hex lattice (6/5/6/5/4=26 circles) as an alternative initial condition alongside the 6-row lattice, plus a final polish pass with tighter ftol=1e-12, to diversify SLSQP start basins
summary.key_insight: The SLSQP optimizer is stuck at a local optimum (sum_radii≈2.6173, score≈0.9929) regardless of initialization diversity — multiple structurally different starts all converge to the same solution
next_directions: Use known near-optimal coordinates from the Packomania database for n=26 as a fixed initial condition, bypassing the hex lattice entirely — this may land in a different basin closer to the global optimum
Try a basin-hopping or simulated annealing approach on top of the best SLSQP solution to escape the current local optimum, using large random perturbations (std>0.05) followed by re-optimization
Switch to a differential evolution or CMA-ES global optimizer instead of SLSQP, which may find a fundamentally different arrangement with higher sum_radii
avoid: Adding more hex-lattice variants (transposed, flipped, 5-row) as starts — all converge to the same local optimum at score 0.9929
Tightening ftol beyond 1e-10 for the polish pass — ftol=1e-12 produced no improvement over ftol=1e-10
Increasing SLSQP maxiter beyond 300 — already tried at 300 with no improvement

reps_annotations.hypothesis: 5x5 grid + 1 gap-filler warm start with SLSQP and analytical jacobians reaches 0.999956 consistently, remains the dominant pattern
